Non-UUID strings pass DTO validation and then reach the service's `isUUID()` check (a secondary gate), but the DTO-level signal is weaker. Additionally, no `@ArrayMaxSize` is present — an attacker can submit thousands of client IDs in a single request, forcing a large `client.count` query in the service.
findings.root_cause: Incomplete validator selection for UUID-typed foreign-key arrays.
findings.impact: Without an ArrayMaxSize cap, excessively large clientIds arrays can cause slow DB queries. The @IsString vs @IsUUID inconsistency reduces early-rejection fidelity and produces less informative 400 error messages.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `@IsString({ each: true })` with `@IsUUID('4', { each: true })` and add `@ArrayMaxSize(50)` (or a project-appropriate cap).
acceptance_criteria: POST /projects with clientIds containing a non-UUID string returns 400 with a UUID validation error
POST /projects with clientIds.length > 50 is rejected with 400

findings.title: create-project.dto.ts: icon field missing @MaxLength
findings.description: The `icon` field has `@IsString` and `@IsOptional` but no `@MaxLength`. It is stored directly in the project row. An unbounded icon string could store multi-KB content.
findings.root_cause: Missing MaxLength decorator.
findings.impact: Low: stored in a single column, but bloats project rows and wastes storage if abused.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `@MaxLength(100)` (icon values are expected to be short identifiers like emoji or icon names).
acceptance_criteria: POST /projects with icon.length > 100 is rejected with 400

findings.title: POST /telework/recurring-rules/generate has no date-range cap — unbounded in-memory expansion
findings.description: The `generateSchedulesFromRules` endpoint accepts arbitrary `startDate`/`endDate` from the DTO and iterates every day between them in memory, building an `expected` Map. There is no cap on the date range span. The `findAll` path (lines 208-213 of the same service) caps the window at 366 days and throws a `BadRequestException` beyond that. A user with `telework:create` can pass e.g. `startDate=2000-01-01, endDate=2099-12-31` (~36500 days × number of active rules) causing a very large in-memory Map and a subsequent `createMany` with tens of thousands of rows.
findings.root_cause: The 366-day guard present in `findAll` was not replicated when `generateSchedulesFromRules` was written or refactored. `GenerateSchedulesDto` has only `@IsDateString()` + `@IsNotEmpty()` — no range constraint.
findings.impact: Authenticated users with `telework:create` (CONTRIBUTEUR and above, scoped to themselves) can trigger a resource-exhaustion event: large in-memory allocation, and a potentially massive `createMany` insert that saturates the DB connection or PostgreSQL write path. With `telework:manage_any`, the payload is multiplied by all users' active rules.
findings.suggested_fix: 1. Add a date-range validation in `GenerateSchedulesDto` or at the top of `generateSchedulesFromRules`: reject if `(end - start) > 366 days`, matching the `findAll` guard. 2. Optionally add `@Throttle` on the controller endpoint.
acceptance_criteria: POST /telework/recurring-rules/generate with a >366-day range returns HTTP 400
POST /telework/recurring-rules/generate with a 90-day range continues to succeed
Unit test covers the range-exceeded path

findings.title: Unbounded date span on `/planning/overview` triggers O(N_days × N_rules) in-memory expansion and uncapped bulk DB write in telework service
findings.description: `PlanningOverviewQueryDto` validates that `startDate` and `endDate` are ISO 8601 strings but imposes no maximum span. `planning.service.ts` passes this range directly to `teleworkService.findForPlanningOverview`, which calls `expandRecurringRulesForRange`. That function iterates day-by-day through the full span (`while (cursorKey <= endKey)`) building an in-memory `Map` of all expected (userId, dateKey) pairs, then calls `prisma.teleworkSchedule.createMany` with no row-count cap. A 10-year span with N active recurring telework rules produces ~3,650 × N Map entries and a single DB write of all missing rows. The route requires only `users:read`, reachable by any authenticated user including OBSERVATEUR-tier roles. The global throttler (30/sec, 600/min) does not mitigate a single expensive request.
findings.root_cause: Missing date-span validation in the DTO (no `@MaxLength`-equivalent for date ranges) combined with an unbounded day-by-day iteration loop in `telework.service.ts` `expandRecurringRulesForRange` (lines 388-401). The planning service calls this indirectly via `findForPlanningOverview` without any guard.
findings.impact: Any authenticated user can send a single request with a multi-year span and cause: (1) event-loop blocking from the synchronous day-by-day iteration through the `expected` Map; (2) an uncapped `createMany` that may insert thousands of `teleworkSchedule` rows, polluting the database. With multiple concurrent requests, this constitutes a DoS. The route is reachable by all roles with `users:read` (the minimum role permission).
findings.suggested_fix: Add a maximum span validation to `PlanningOverviewQueryDto` (e.g., 90 or 366 days). Example: after `@IsISO8601()`, add a custom `@MaxDateSpanDays(366)` validator or a class-level `@ValidateIf` that checks `endDate - startDate <= 366 days`. Also add a guard in `expandRecurringRulesForRange` that clamps or refuses spans above a threshold to defend in depth.
acceptance_criteria: A request to `GET /planning/overview` with `startDate` and `endDate` more than 366 days apart returns HTTP 400
Requests within the allowed span continue to work correctly
The span validation is enforced in the DTO (not just the service) so it applies before service execution
findings.notes: Cross-reference: telework.service.ts expandRecurringRulesForRange lines 388-401 (day-by-day loop) and lines 430-440 (uncapped createMany). Planning service call at planning.service.ts findForPlanningOverview. The loop is per rule, so the total iterations are N_rules × N_days.

verified_clean: main.ts — helmet CSP configured (defaultSrc self, scriptSrc self, imgSrc self/data/blob), CORS via resolveAllowedOrigins(), 1 MiB body limit, auth guard registration order (ThrottlerBehindProxyGuard → JwtAuthGuard → ForcePasswordChangeGuard → PermissionsGuardV2), production boot assertions for JWT_SECRET, AUDIT_HASH_KEY, RBAC_GUARD_MODE, AUTH_EXPOSE_RESET_TOKEN
main.ts — Swagger protected by timing-safe HTTP Basic Auth (timingSafeEqual via safeEqual()); production boot throws if SWAGGER_USER/SWAGGER_PASS absent when Swagger enabled
main.ts — ValidationPipe(whitelist:true, forbidNonWhitelisted:true, transform:true) registered globally

auth/auth.controller.ts — all routes guarded: login/@Public, register/@Public, refresh/@Public (with Throttle), logout/@AllowSelfService, profile/@AllowSelfService, me/@AllowSelfService, me/permissions/@AllowSelfService, reset-password-token/@RequirePermissions, reset-password/@Public+Throttle
auth/auth.controller.ts — refresh token delivered via HttpOnly + SameSite=Strict cookie; production uses __Host- prefix (Secure implied); cookie name is env-symmetric (refreshCookieName)

auth/auth.service.ts — validateUser: disabled accounts return null (not distinct error), indistinguishable from bad credentials (same timing via bcrypt.compare before isActive check); SEC-005 differential neutralization
auth/auth.service.ts — generateResetToken: token stored as SHA-256 hash, never stored in plain; raw token only exposed if AUTH_EXPOSE_RESET_TOKEN=true AND NODE_ENV != production
auth/auth.service.ts — resetPassword: uses serializable transaction with CAS (updateMany where usedAt=null) to prevent TOCTOU; revokeAllForUser + jwtNotBefore.bumpUser on reset
auth/auth.service.ts — register: behind REGISTRATION_ENABLED gate (default false); REGISTRATION_EMAIL_DOMAIN allowlist supported

auth/refresh-token.service.ts — token stored as SHA-256 hash (never plain); rotate() uses serializable transaction; reuse detection (revoke entire family if revoked token presented); 48-byte random token (crypto.randomBytes)
auth/jwt-blacklist.service.ts — fail-CLOSED on both write (blacklist) and read (isBlacklisted) — Redis failure returns 503 on blacklist, true (blocked) on check
auth/jwt-not-before.service.ts — fail-OPEN on read (correctly documented; jti blacklist provides primary protection); bumpUser stores floor(now/1000)+1 with +1 same-second guard
auth/login-lockout.service.ts — per-(account,IP) progressive lockout with SHA-256 keyed identifier; fail-OPEN (does not block logins on Redis outage); LoginDto.login lowercased in keyId()
auth/guards/jwt-auth.guard.ts — honours @Public() decorator; delegates to passport-jwt which enforces ignoreExpiration:false
auth/guards/force-password-change.guard.ts — reads live DB flag (request.user.forcePasswordChange re-hydrated on every request by JwtStrategy), not stale token claim
auth/guards/throttler-behind-proxy.guard.ts — uses clientIp() (req.ip, not req.ips[0]) for correct real-client IP under Fastify+trustProxy
auth/strategies/jwt.strategy.ts — validates jti blacklist (fail-closed) then nbf gate then DB user lookup; rejects inactive users; attaches jti+exp to request.user for logout blacklisting
auth/dto/login.dto.ts — @MaxLength(1024) on password (SEC-003); @MaxLength(254) on login
auth/dto/register.dto.ts — @IsStrongPassword() on password; @MaxLength on email, login, firstName, lastName; NO_CONTROL_CHARS regex on firstName/lastName
auth/dto/refresh-token.dto.ts — @MaxLength(256) on optional refreshToken field
auth/dto/reset-password-token.dto.ts — @IsUUID() on userId; strict type enforcement
rbac/permissions.guard.ts — zero-trust enforce mode by default; @Public/@AllowSelfService/@RequirePermissions/@RequireAnyPermission required; production boot rejects RBAC_GUARD_MODE=permissive

rbac/permissions.service.ts — RBAC V4: permissions resolved from in-memory ROLE_TEMPLATES via DB templateKey lookup; Redis cache with negative TTL; no DB-stored per-role permission overrides; singleflight coalescing
rbac/roles.controller.ts — all routes protected by @RequirePermissions('users:manage_roles'); ParseUUIDPipe on :id param
rbac/roles.service.ts — isSystem roles are non-mutable (403); templateKey immutable after creation; isDefault singleton maintained in serializable transaction
